title: Canonical IR
description: The backend-agnostic transcript contract (ADR-0006). One document = one recording (batch) or one session epoch (ADR-0009). Both the modular and joint backends (ADR-0005) emit this; all renderers consume it.

schema_version.description: IR schema version. Bump on breaking changes.

kind.description: batch = a file/URL; session = a live capture session epoch (ADR-0009).

uri.description: Origin of the audio (file path, URL). Absent for ephemeral live captures.

sha256.description: Hash of the normalized source audio, for provenance/dedup.

diarizer.description: Diarizer id, e.g. 'pyannote/community-1'. Omitted for a joint backend that does ASR+diarization in one model.

speakers.description: Canonical, recording-wide speaker identities. Stable IDs come from global diarization (ADR-0005); names/roles come from the canonicalization mapping (ADR-0006).

glossary.description: Term normalization applied across the document (ADR-0006).

display_name.description: Human name/label, e.g. 'Aaron'. Absent if unresolved.

evidence.description: Why this label was assigned, e.g. 'self-intro @ 12.4s'.

source.description: How the label was set: llm mapping, human override, or non-LLM fallback (plain Speaker A/B/C).

t.description: Word start time (s), absolute within the document.

start.description: Turn start (s), absolute within the document.

end.description: Turn end (s), absolute. Must be >= start (enforced by the apply() builder).

words.description: Optional word-level timing (present when the backend provides alignment).

secondary_speakers.description: Other speakers active during this turn (overlapped speech).

chunk.description: Index of the ASR chunk this turn came from.

offset_s.description: Absolute start time of that chunk; carries true offsets so stitching can't drift (ADR-0006).
